title: fix: return 400 for category-mismatched models on wrong endpoint
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Right model, wrong door
gist.blurb: Ever tried asking an image model to write a poem? We now politely point you to the right endpoint instead of throwing a confusing 502 error.
gist.summary: Added validation to ensure requested models match the endpoint's service category, returning a helpful 400 error instead of a 502.
gist.impact: Developers will now receive clear error messages when sending requests to the wrong endpoint, and our analytics will no longer be polluted by these mismatched requests.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a helpful guide or traffic controller, directing a confused monitor-headed robot (holding an image) to the correct door. Represents routing models to the correct endpoints.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny guide hat, politely pointing a chunky monitor-headed robot toward a glowing door. The robot is holding a picture frame. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny guide hat, politely pointing a chunky monitor-headed robot toward a glowing door. The robot is holding a picture frame. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
